conversations.content: Let's do some economics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Calculate using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier: A batch of rare herbs is consigned at the auction house with a transaction price S=1200 gold coins and an auction duration T=48 hours. What is the actual handling fee F?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier, given the transaction price S and auction duration T, find the actual deducted handling fee F</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:71] Time: 2025-07-27-10:05, Content: The user explained to the AI the auction house handling fee calculation rule in the world of Azeroth. Its core formula is: The actual handling fee F equals the item's transaction price S multiplied by the base handling fee rate 0.05, and then multiplied by (1 + T/12), where T is in hours. That is, F = S × 0.05 × (1 + T/12). And confirmed that this formula can be used to find the actual deducted handling fee F when the transaction price S and auction duration T are known. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 relevant entry, [mem-id:71] gives F = S × 0.05 × (1 + T/12). Given S=1200, T=48, then F = 1200 × 0.05 × (1 + 48/12) = 60 × 5 = 300.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the actual handling fee F is 300 gold coins.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
